summary: Iteration 49 (a+b+c follow-on) — OG share HTML, directory has_public_library chip, shelf-overlap badge & banner. 12/12 backend pytest cases pass. Frontend playwright tests confirm: anon visitor sees no overlap banner, signed-in user with overlap sees banner with correct count + per-book 'You have this' badge, directory shows 📚 Library chip linking to /u/<handle>/library for opted-in users.

minor.issue: When anon viewer hits the endpoint, books are returned WITHOUT a you_also_have field (which is the documented intent). When signed-in viewer matches owner, viewer_is_signed_in is correctly set to False but the field name 'viewer_is_signed_in' is semantically inaccurate (caller IS signed in, they're just the owner). Consider renaming to viewer_is_other_signed_in or computing_overlap for clarity. Not blocking — frontend already branches correctly.

issues: Copy reads 'You have 1 of these book too.' when overlap_count===1 — 'of these book' is grammatically off. Suggest 'You have this book too' or 'You have 1 book in common.'

action_items: Minor copy: fix singular form in PublicLibraryView.jsx overlap banner — 'of these book' reads awkwardly when count===1 (line ~166). Suggest 'You have 1 book in common.' / 'You have N of these books too.' for plural.
Consider clarifying naming of viewer_is_signed_in (which is actually 'viewer-is-different-signed-in-user'). Optional, doesn't block.
critical_code_review_comments: OG share endpoint correctly mirrors 404 invariant for not-opted-in vs nonexistent handle (verified — identical responses).
users_directory uses {'$nin': [null, '']} fix to exclude empty/null usernames — regression-safe, confirmed.
Overlap match is case-insensitive on lower(title)|lower(author) — handled by stripping & lowercasing on both sides server-side.
Owner-self check (viewer.user_id != owner.user_id) correctly suppresses overlap computation.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (3/3 testable flows; 4th verified via backend invariant since UI conditional is purely server-driven)

test_credentials: Opted-in user: TEST_fe_opted_e24c9d@example.com / hunter2pw! / @febopta508d. Overlap test user (newly created with seeded matching book): saved to /tmp/fe_overlap_user.json.
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_fe_overlap_<uuid>@example.com user with one book matching opted user's 'Harry Potter Sorcerer Stone Test' by 'J. K. Rowling' to verify the overlap flow end-to-end. Stored at /tmp/fe_overlap_user.json.
context_for_next_testing_agent: iteration_49 features verified end-to-end. /tmp/fe_users.json + /tmp/fe_overlap_user.json carry test creds. Backend test file lives at /app/tests/test_iteration_49.py (note: NOT under /app/backend/tests because watchfiles reloads when files there change; the dir under /app/tests is safe). Public-library endpoint now branches on auth: anon → overlap_count=0, viewer_is_signed_in=False, no you_also_have field; signed-in non-owner → real overlap_count + you_also_have flags; signed-in owner → overlap_count=0, viewer_is_signed_in=False (designed not to compute self-overlap).
rca of the issue: N/A — no functional bugs found. Only one minor copy-grammar issue noted.
